surface: AI factories are industrial infrastructure of the AI era
why_it_matters: Core thesis surface for Chapter 15 and the book's hardware middle.
allowed_caption: NVIDIA framed AI factories as industrial infrastructure, with inference as workload, tokens as commodity, and compute as revenue.
blocked_claims: No neutral industry definition, independent economics, deployed-factory performance, or revenue proof.

surface: DSX AI Factory Platform across chips, systems, facilities, libraries, APIs, software, reference designs, and methodologies
why_it_matters: Best bridge from NVIDIA stagecraft to Chapter 16 physical constraints.
allowed_caption: NVIDIA framed DSX as an AI factory platform spanning hardware, facilities, software, cooling, and power.
blocked_claims: No customer deployment, adoption scale, facility performance, energy efficiency, or proof that DSX solves power/cooling/interconnection.

surface: Seven chips, five rack systems, and one-gigawatt system comparison
why_it_matters: Strongest single surface for NVIDIA's system-level AI factory argument.
allowed_caption: NVIDIA presented Vera Rubin as a multi-chip, multi-rack AI factory system and compared it to an X86 plus Hopper factory frame.
blocked_claims: No exact ratios, token throughput, revenue, deployed capacity, or neutral performance proof without independent corroboration.

surface: 2024/2026/2028 roadmap with Blackwell, Rubin, Feynman and networking/storage systems
why_it_matters: Best chronology visual for showing GTC as cadence and future promise.
allowed_caption: NVIDIA showed a roadmap spanning Blackwell, Rubin, Feynman, networking, storage, and rack-scale systems.
blocked_claims: No future roadmap item as happened history, guaranteed delivery, production status, specs, or timing.

surface: Vera Rubin unlocks 150B revenue opportunity; annual revenue per gigawatt comparison
why_it_matters: High narrative value but the riskiest economics slide.
allowed_caption: NVIDIA connected AI factory infrastructure to annual revenue opportunity and gigawatt-scale economics.
blocked_claims: No independent revenue opportunity, actual revenue, ROI, deployed capacity, customer economics, or neutral valuation.

surface: Inference performance and efficiency drive company results; throughput per MW and model tiers
why_it_matters: Useful because it links tokens, megawatts, and model tiers more directly than product renders.
allowed_caption: NVIDIA framed inference throughput and efficiency as business-relevant AI factory metrics.
blocked_claims: No exact TPS/MW values, company result proof, cost-per-token, energy-per-token, or model-rank inference without extraction and corroboration.

why_it_matters: Good setup for reasoning/test-time compute and Chapter 15's inference turn.
allowed_caption: NVIDIA framed inference compute as entering a new scaling phase.
blocked_claims: No verified 10000X claim, product demand proof, model behavior proof, or neutral compute requirement.

surface: Vera Rubin NVL72 launch partners and rack performance claims
why_it_matters: Useful only if the chapter needs a concrete 2026 rack-system announcement.
allowed_caption: NVIDIA announced Vera Rubin NVL72 with launch-partner and platform claims.
blocked_claims: No neutral partner proof, performance-per-watt, NVFP4, HBM4, NVLink6, production, or availability claim.

surface: AI inference compute / Groq 3 LPX roadmap and 2H26 availability
why_it_matters: Lower narrative value than page 50 roadmap and page 49 system comparison.
allowed_caption: NVIDIA showed an inference-compute roadmap including Groq 3 LPX and 2H26 availability language.
blocked_claims: No shipped availability, exact specs, performance, product naming beyond slide context, or independent roadmap proof.

surface: Rubin GPU and Groq 3 LPU comparison; SRAM, HBM4, FLOPS, transistors
why_it_matters: Can support Chapter 14 technical detail but risks spec-sheet clutter.
allowed_caption: NVIDIA presented hardware detail comparing Rubin GPU and Groq 3 LPU characteristics.
blocked_claims: No verified specs, product availability, performance rank, chip comparison, or accelerator superiority.

surface: DSX Max-Q, Flex, Exchange, Sim deployment framing
why_it_matters: Secondary DSX detail; page 51 already carries the platform frame.
allowed_caption: NVIDIA described DSX variants or components as part of scalable AI factory deployment framing.
blocked_claims: No accelerated deployment, energy efficiency, customer outcome, facility benefit, adoption, or performance proof.

surface: NVIDIA AI Platform ecosystem map: AI natives, inference, agent frameworks, protocols, model builders
why_it_matters: Useful if Chapter 15 needs platform breadth beyond hardware.
allowed_caption: NVIDIA presented an AI platform map spanning inference, agent frameworks, protocols, model builders, and vertical AI categories.
blocked_claims: No ecosystem dominance, adoption, standardization, robotics relevance, or category completeness claim.

surface: Agents as a new computing platform
why_it_matters: Good bridge to tool/coding-agent chapters, but not core to GTC visual sequence.
allowed_caption: NVIDIA framed agents as a new computing platform.
blocked_claims: No agent autonomy, productivity, adoption, safety, or platform dominance claim.

why_it_matters: Too risky as a benchmark visual without independent rows.
allowed_caption: NVIDIA showed Nemotron benchmark-related claims and benchmark names.
blocked_claims: No live rank, best-open-model claim, benchmark superiority, model capability, or comparison to GLM/Kimi without triangulation.

surface: NVIDIA leaderboard-topping open models plus physical AI and robotics categories
why_it_matters: Contains too much non-LLM and robotics material for this book's spine.
allowed_caption: NVIDIA grouped open models and other AI categories in one source surface.
blocked_claims: No robotics expansion, physical-AI chapter drift, leaderboard claim, or broad model superiority.
